api_contracts.name: Patient Registration API
api_contracts.error_handling: Circuit breaker with 3 failures/30s threshold, fallback to cached data for reads, retry with exponential backoff for writes

api_contracts.name: Scheduling API
api_contracts.error_handling: Optimistic locking for concurrent scheduling, conflict resolution with user notification

api_contracts.name: Billing API
api_contracts.error_handling: Transaction rollback on failure, idempotent POST operations, dead letter queue for failed claim submissions

api_contracts.name: Pharmacy API
api_contracts.error_handling: Strict validation for controlled substances, drug interaction check before order confirmation, audit trail for all pharmacy operations

api_contracts.name: Lab Orders API
api_contracts.error_handling: Order validation against test catalog, result notification via WebSocket for real-time updates

api_contracts.name: Clinical Documentation API
api_contracts.error_handling: Document locking for concurrent edits, version conflict resolution, auto-save with draft status

api_contracts.name: IBMi Data Access Layer (DAL)
api_contracts.rate_limit: Connection pool: 50 concurrent connections to IBMi
api_contracts.error_handling: Connection pool management, automatic reconnect on IBMi session timeout, circuit breaker at 5 failures/60s, graceful degradation to read-only mode

api_contracts.name: IAM / Authentication API
api_contracts.error_handling: Account lockout after 5 failed attempts, MFA fallback to backup codes, session invalidation on suspicious activity

api_contracts.name: Audit Logging API
api_contracts.rate_limit: No rate limit (fire-and-forget with async queue)
api_contracts.error_handling: Async message queue (never block application flow), dead letter queue for failed writes, guaranteed delivery via persistent queue

data_flow_mappings.name: Patient Record: IBMi → Web UI

field_mappings.transform: Unpack decimal, zero-pad to 10 digits, prefix 'P-'
Decrypt, extract last 4 digits, re-encrypt at rest

validation_rules: Patient ID must be non-null and unique
DOB must be valid date and not in future
SSN handled with field-level encryption — never transmitted in plaintext
MRN format validated against hospital numbering scheme

data_flow_mappings.name: Scheduling Data: IBMi ↔ Web UI

field_mappings.transform: Map facility code to facility name via lookup

validation_rules: Appointment date must not be in the past for new bookings
Provider must be active and credentialed at specified facility

data_flow_mappings.name: Pharmacy Orders: IBMi ↔ Web UI

field_mappings.transform: Map legacy codes to standard SIG codes

validation_rules: Controlled substance orders require prescriber DEA verification
Drug interaction check mandatory before order commit
Dosage range validation against drug formulary

data_flow_mappings.name: User Role Mapping: IdP → IAM → Application

field_mappings.transform: Map IdP groups to application roles via role mapping table
Map to clinical role enum (physician, nurse, pharmacist, admin, billing)

validation_rules: Every user must have at least one mapped role
Clinical roles must have corresponding credential verification
PHI access requires explicit role grant — no default access

migration_strategy.source_system: IBM AS/400 (IBMi) with 5250 green screen terminals
migration_strategy.target_system: Modern web application with API gateway wrapping IBMi backend

data_migration.approach: No bulk data migration required — IBMi remains the system of record. The modern UI reads/writes through the API gateway to the same DB2/400 data. Reference data (lookup tables, code mappings) cached in modern application layer with TTL-based refresh.
data_migration.volume: 2M+ patient records remain in DB2/400. Only reference data and user/role configuration migrated to modern stores.
data_migration.downtime_plan: Zero-downtime migration. Green screens and modern UI operate in parallel. Module-by-module cutover with rollback capability.

migration_strategy.rollback_plan: Each module has independent rollback: re-enable green screen access for that module, disable modern UI routing. Green screens remain operational throughout migration period. Full rollback to pre-modernization state possible within 4 hours by reverting API gateway routing.

legacy_bridging.description: Facade pattern via the IBMi Data Access Layer (API-007). Provides a simplified REST interface over complex RPG program calls and DB2/400 data access. Translates between EBCDIC/packed decimal IBMi formats and modern JSON/UTF-8. Handles connection pooling, session management, and EBCDIC character set conversion.
legacy_bridging.lifespan: Permanent — IBMi remains the system of record. Facade evolves as RPG programs are gradually refactored or wrapped.

legacy_bridging.description: Anti-corruption layer isolates the modern UI domain model from legacy 5250 screen-flow conventions. Legacy green screen applications use screen-based navigation with function keys (F3=Exit, F5=Refresh, etc.) and fixed-format data entry. The ACL maps these into modern REST API calls with proper domain objects, preventing legacy UI paradigms from leaking into the modern application.
legacy_bridging.lifespan: Until all green screen modules are replaced (12-18 months), then ACL simplified to direct API calls.

legacy_bridging.description: Adapter pattern for DB2/400 data access via IBM i Access ODBC/JDBC drivers. Handles EBCDIC-to-UTF-8 conversion, packed decimal unpacking, fixed-length field trimming, and date format normalization. Connection pooling with automatic reconnect on IBMi session timeout.
legacy_bridging.lifespan: Permanent — DB2/400 remains the data store. Adapter maintained as long as IBMi is in production.

legacy_bridging.description: Adapter pattern for JD Edwards file-based data transfer. Currently uses flat file exports. Adapter reads files, parses fixed-format records, and maps to modern billing data model. Future phase may upgrade to JD Edwards REST API if available.
legacy_bridging.lifespan: Until JD Edwards integration is upgraded to API-based (Phase 2+).

legacy_bridging.description: Facade provides monitoring visibility into COBOL batch job execution without modifying the jobs themselves. Batch jobs continue running on IBMi nightly schedule. Facade captures job status, completion time, and error codes for the modern monitoring dashboard.
legacy_bridging.lifespan: Permanent — COBOL batch jobs remain unchanged per scope boundaries.

stages.name: Code Quality
stages.description: Linting, formatting, static analysis for TypeScript/JavaScript frontend and API code

stages.name: Testing
stages.description: Unit tests (>80% coverage), integration tests against IBMi test environment, E2E tests for critical clinical workflows

stages.name: Security Scanning
stages.description: SAST, dependency vulnerability scanning, secret detection (critical for PHI/HIPAA compliance)

stages.name: Build
stages.description: Containerized builds with versioned tags, SBOM generation

stages.name: Deploy
stages.description: Immutable deployments to staging → production with blue-green strategy, health check gates

stages.name: Monitor
stages.description: Post-deploy health checks, rollback triggers on error rate spike, PHI access anomaly detection

automation.dependency_audit: Weekly automated scan of all dependencies
automation.branch_cleanup: Auto-delete merged feature branches after 7 days
automation.hipaa_compliance_check: Pre-merge check for PHI exposure in code, configs, and logs

phase_1.name: Foundation + Patient Registration (MVP)
scope: API gateway foundation and IBMi DAL (API-007)
IAM service (API-008) with SSO and MFA
Patient registration module (API-001) — highest visibility, moderate complexity
validation_criteria: Patient registration works end-to-end via modern UI
SSO login with MFA successfully authenticates against IdP
All PHI access logged in audit trail
Green screen and modern UI operate in parallel for registration
HIPAA compliance verified for patient registration workflow

phase_2.name: Full API Integration + Remaining Modules
scope: Billing module (API-003) with JD Edwards integration
Pharmacy module (API-004) with drug interaction checking
validation_criteria: All 6 clinical modules functional in modern UI
Mobile access validated by nursing staff
Parallel run: green screen and modern UI producing identical results
User satisfaction score ≥ 7.0 in pilot group

api_gateway.identity: Dedicated service account for API gateway
api_gateway.permissions: Route requests, validate tokens, rate limit

ibmi_dal.identity: Dedicated IBMi service profile with restricted library access
ibmi_dal.permissions: Execute specific RPG programs, read/write specific DB2/400 tables

iam_service.identity: Dedicated service account for identity management
iam_service.permissions: Validate tokens, issue tokens, manage sessions, read user directory

audit_service.identity: Dedicated write-only service account for audit logging
audit_service.permissions: Append-only write to audit log store

service_security.inter_service_auth: Mutual TLS (mTLS) for all internal service-to-service communication
service_security.audit_trail: Every service call logged: caller identity, target service, action, result, timestamp, correlation ID

circuit_breaker.threshold: 5 failures in 60 seconds triggers open state
circuit_breaker.fallback: Return cached data for reads, queue writes for retry

applies_to: All write operations via IBMi DAL

dead_letter_queue.purpose: Capture failed operations for manual review and replay
dead_letter_queue.alerting: PagerDuty alert on DLQ depth > 10 messages
